Inglis Great Southern Sale: Five things we learned on Day 1

Racing Victoria has unveiled a new look to VOBIS launching in the 2026/27 racing season, where it will be the only jurisdiction to reward bonuses to owners, trainers, breeders, and jockeys with every winner. TTR examined the bonus schemes offered by each state and territory to work out where could be most lucrative to race.

Inglis will unveil a new online selling concept next week, with G1 Goodwood Handicap winner Reserve Bank the first horse offered through the ‘Bull Ring’, a private platform designed to facilitate high end private sales in an auction environment.
At the Inglis Great Southern Weanling Sale, Ben and Ryan Foote's New Zealand-based St Kilda Lodge walked away with an outstanding value buy, after securing a strong son of Native Trail for $20,000.

Thursday morning heralds the start of the Inglis Great Southern Sale, returning in 2026 with a bumper two-day catalogue of weanlings in Melbourne. Sebastian Hutch hopes to maintain the Inglis sale series' momentum into their last event of the season.

Celestial Charm was the headline act on the Kensington, scoring impressively, while Royal Air Force and Jarrito also scored smart wins at the same venue. Down in Melbourne, Tisseyre headed a Maurice quinella in testing conditions at Sale.

La Mexicana was the first offering for Peter O’Brien Bloodstock and she rewarded the vendor by headlining the latest Inglis Online Sale at $920,000. The high class mare will head to Blue Gum Farm. There was plenty more action with the sale aggregating $3,915,100.

Less than a decade after buying Cambridge Stud, Sir Brendan and Lady Jo Lindsay find themselves on racing’s biggest stage. It started when Henry Plumptre put a rare nine-and-a-half rating on the page of a $950,000 Easter yearling and urged them to keep bidding. Fireball Miss became just the fourth filly to complete the G2 The Roses and G1 Queensland Oaks double this century, while meetings at Eagle Farm, Flemington and Randwick provided plenty for form analyst Nic Ashman to dissect.
